description: Attackers use targeted phishing emails with malicious attachments (archives containing executable files, DLLs, LNK files, and decoy PDFs) to gain initial access.
name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment

description: Victims are required to unpack an archive and execute an EXE or LNK file to initiate the compromise process.
name: User Execution: Malicious File

description: Attackers use COM hijacking for persistence by registering malicious DLLs under specific CLSIDs in the Windows registry.
name: Component Object Model Hijacking

description: Attackers use DLL side-loading to execute malicious code by loading a malicious DLL through a legitimate executable.
name: DLL Side-Loading

description: Attackers obfuscate code and configuration data using techniques like Base36, Base64, RC4 encryption, and VMProtect packing.
name: Obfuscated Files or Information

description: TunnelRAT uses DNS tunneling for C2 communication, including custom domain generation and encoding.
name: Application Layer Protocol: DNS

description: Malware uses HTTPS and HTTP for C2 communication.
name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols

description: EmojiRAT uses Telegram Bot API for bidirectional C2 communication.
name: Web Service: Bidirectional Communication

description: Malware collects information about files and directories on the compromised host.
name: File and Directory Discovery

description: Malware collects system information such as domain name, computer name, username, and IP addresses.
name: System Information Discovery

description: TunnelRAT and EmojiRAT exfiltrate data over their C2 communication channels.
name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel

description: EmojiRAT encodes data using a custom emoji symbol table for C2 communication.
name: Data Encoding: Non-Standard Encoding

description: TunnelRAT encodes data using Base36 and SSF client uses Base64 encoding for configuration data.
name: Data Encoding: Standard Encoding

description: Attackers set the 'hidden' attribute for loader DLL files to evade detection.
name: Hide Artifacts: Hidden Files and Directories

description: Attackers use DLL side-loading to hijack execution flow via legitimate executables.
name: Hijack Execution Flow: DLL

description: Loader and reconnaissance malware check for specific substrings in the USERDOMAIN environment variable to evade analysis.
name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion: System Checks

description: Loader second stage checks for certain command-line arguments and deletes its own DLL file if found.
name: Indicator Removal: File Deletion

description: Loader second stage checks for command-line arguments and removes COM object registry values used for persistence.
name: Indicator Removal: Clear Persistence

description: TunnelRAT uses custom algorithms to generate domains for DNS-based C2 communication.
name: Dynamic Resolution: Domain Generation Algorithms

description: Modified SSF client uses TLS for encrypted C2 channels.
name: Encrypted Channel: Asymmetric Cryptography

description: Modified SSF client enables SOCKS proxy functionality for attackers.
name: Proxy

description: TunnelRAT and EmojiRAT are used to upload files to the compromised host.
name: Ingress Tool Transfer
